narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because geopolitical de-escalation from US-Iran deal hopes is triggering broad dollar selling with DXY at 97.77 down 0.72% and all USD pairs moving in concert.
2. JPY intervention pressure because suspected BOJ action is suppressing yen crosses even as risk-on conditions would normally lift them, creating a direct conflict with the JPY cross sell signals on USDJPY, EURJPY, and GBPJPY.
3. Commodity and risk-on momentum because copper up 4%, silver up 6.46%, and S&P500 up 0.81% are confirming a genuine reflation bid that is disproportionately lifting AUD, NZD, and CHF against the dollar.

PRICE CONFIRMS

NZDUSD is the cleanest expression of the dominant narrative with a strong H4 buy signal of plus 6, NZD ranked top in both D1 and H4 CSM at 65 and 100 respectively, and the pair confirming USD weakness with commodity support. EURUSD and GBPUSD moving in near lockstep at 0.94 correlation confirms the dollar is the common factor being sold rather than individual currency strength driving divergence. AUDUSD is a notable laggard given AUD shows fading D1 CSM strength from 100 to 79 on H4, suggesting commodity gains are partly priced and AUD is losing relative momentum to NZD.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed. VIX at 16.19 down 6.85% and S&P500 up 0.81% together confirm genuine risk appetite, though gold surging 3.44% introduces a partial safe-haven overlay that reflects residual geopolitical uncertainty rather than contradicting the primary risk-on read.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on regime: Gold at 4712.60 up 3.44% historically signals fear or dollar collapse, yet equities and copper simultaneously surge, suggesting gold is trading as a USD depreciation hedge rather than a safe-haven, but the signal remains structurally ambiguous.
2. JPY crosses versus risk-on conditions: USDJPY, EURJPY, and GBPJPY all carry H4 sell signals despite a confirmed risk-on environment where yen crosses would typically rise, with BOJ intervention suspicion the only coherent explanation but unconfirmed officially.

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: NZDUSD up - NZD CSM accelerating, USD weakest, strong H4 buy signal, commodity tailwind - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDJPY up - NZD strength dominates over JPY intervention risk, H4 buy confirmed - Conviction 6/10
Avoid: AUDJPY - fading AUD momentum meets unresolved BOJ intervention pressure creating directional noise.
Watch: DXY breaking and holding below 97.50 confirms primary USD sell and invalidates any dollar stabilization narrative.

themes.theme: US-Iran peace deal hopes dominating risk sentiment
USD weakness on geopolitical de-escalation and risk-on mood
JPY strength from suspected BOJ intervention
Commodity rally on lower geopolitical risk premium
Gold and silver surge amid USD depreciation
Eurozone stagflation concerns persist despite risk-on flows
Central bank hold expectations with modest hawkish tilts in EM
Risk appetite supporting higher-yielding and commodity currencies

key_observation: US-Iran peace deal hopes are the dominant driver, causing broad USD weakness as risk appetite surges. DXY breaks below 98.00, USD/JPY declines sharply despite BOJ intervention concerns, and commodity-linked currencies rally strongly. Oil retreats on reduced supply risk, while precious metals spike on weaker dollar. Equity futures advance on de-escalation optimism.
watch: ADP employment data for April private-sector hiring trends; any reversal in US-Iran deal optimism could quickly reverse these risk-on flows; BOJ intervention signals and JPY support levels; oil price stabilization as geopolitical premium unwinds; USD positioning for potential reversal if deal collapses per Trump ultimatum
